rows.description: Soluta varia. 1700.
rows.notes: Clear centered page title at top of the folio, no monetary columns present on the same line.

rows.description: Act Dinner
rows.notes: Description is legible. I do not see a clearly aligned amount on this line (small marginal marks just above might belong to the next line); treated as an entry with no amount visible.

rows.description: Money baggs
rows.notes: Short description clearly readable. There is no unambiguous value in the pounds/shillings/pence columns opposite this line; likely a small mark but not a clear amount.

rows.description: To ye Gardiner for a days work and nails abv ye back yard of ye Coll.
rows.notes: There is a small amount visible in the rightmost columns opposite this row. The alignment suggests 0 pounds, 2 shillings, 6 pence; the digits are a little faint, so confidence is moderate.

rows.description: To James Davyes Joyner & bill
rows.notes: Handwriting of amount is faint but aligned in the column set; read as 0 pounds, 2 shillings, 6 pence. There is some ink blur making the pence digit slightly uncertain.

rows.description: To ye Widdow Vaughan by consent
rows.notes: Pounds (4) and shillings (13) are clearly written and well aligned; pence column is 0. Slight flourish on the '4' of pounds but placement is unambiguous.

rows.description: To Groves for fewell, oatmeal &c
rows.notes: Amount appears as 0 pounds, 5 shillings, 0 pence. Numerals are well aligned and pass validation.

rows.description: To Rich. Lumby
rows.notes: Amount reads as '4 6' in the shilling/pence columns (4s 6d). The pounds column is blank/zero; digits are distinct.

rows.description: 2 Buttery books
rows.notes: The pounds column shows '1' and the shilling column '16'. The handwriting for the shillings is slightly crowded but reads as 16s, which is within 0–19, so accepted.

rows.description: Paid mr Osborne for Wells mapps
rows.notes: Pounds '1' and shillings '4' are clearly visible and aligned; pence column blank/zero.

rows.description: To Dr Levitt for Binding Coopers Anatomy
rows.notes: Shillings read as '12' and pounds column blank. The '12' is clear and valid (≤19).

rows.description: To mr Pain Brazier
rows.notes: Shillings read '8', pounds blank; numerals are distinct and well aligned.

rows.description: To Groves ye cook for xmas new years day, epiphany
rows.notes: The line shows '08' in the pounds column and '16' in the shillings column. While 16 shillings is valid, the pounds digit has a slight flourish; read as £8 16s 0d with moderate confidence.

rows.description: To mr Burghers for engraving ye front of ye Coll.
rows.notes: Pounds '11' and shillings '8' are both legible and aligned; engraving costs plausibly higher, supporting the reading of pounds rather than shillings only.

rows.description: For two basses for ye chappell
rows.notes: Amount reads clearly as 5 pounds, 7 shillings, 6 pence in the three columns; digits are well separated and pass validation (pence 6 ≤11).

rows.description: To mr Subrectr. Adams for preaching for mr vivian
rows.notes: Shillings '11' is readable; pounds blank. The '11' is clearly inside the shilling column.

rows.description: To Day ye Smith
rows.notes: Shillings '3' and pence '4' distinct and aligned. Slight ink blot near description but numeric columns are clear.

rows.description: For { George her taxe Ann. 6r. mich. 4r }
rows.notes: This is the first line of a braced tax pair. Pounds '2' and shillings '4' are readable though the bracket and script crowd the amount area slightly.

rows.description: For { Jennings taxe Ann. 6r. mich. 4r }
rows.notes: Second line of the braced tax group. Pounds '1' is clear; shillings/pence read as zero. Ink for the brace slightly interferes but reading is reasonable.

rows.description: To James Fifield selater
rows.notes: Pounds read as '07', shillings '3' and pence '7' appear, but the pence digit is slightly cramped and could be another single digit; chosen reading fits column alignment and is plausible.

rows.description: To my Howard at ye recommendation of ye ArchBp of Dublyn
rows.notes: Shillings '5' is visible; small amount and clear alignment though the handwriting is compact.

rows.description: To Cosens for candlys Apr. 8th 14.0. Octob. 4th 15.6
rows.notes: This line shows a larger amount; pounds '13', shillings '9', pence '6' are clearly written and align well with the three ruling columns. The detailed dates in the description corroborate a sum of multiple items.

rows.description: To Broadwater for candly for ye butterry
rows.notes: Reads as £5 12s 0d; digits are distinct and fall into valid ranges (12 shillings < 20).

rows.description: To mr Hall for printing ye cutts of ye Coll. Front.
rows.notes: Pounds '1' and shillings '10' are legible and well aligned; pence blank.

rows.description: To mr Nicoll recommended by ye D. of Orm. by consent
rows.notes: Amount reads as £2 3s 0d; the '2' and '3' are clear. There is a slight ink flourish on the 'N' of Nicoll but it does not affect the numeric columns.

rows.description: To mr clark for 20.subscription parchm'ts
rows.notes: Pounds '1' is clear; shillings/pence read as zero. The word 'subscription' is slightly abbreviated but the amount column is distinct.

rows.description: 9 yards of flaxen cloth for towells
rows.notes: Shillings '9' clearly visible in the shilling column; pounds blank.

rows.description: To mr Pontell a French pretest. Abbot by consent
rows.notes: Amount reads as £1 1s 0d; both digits are distinct and unambiguous.

rows.description: To Burrows Sext'r. ye young ye smith for mending ye lock and two keys to ye library
rows.notes: Shillings '5' is clearly visible; the descriptive line is densely written but numeric columns are clear.

rows.description: Wine for ye Hall Oct. 5th
rows.notes: Shillings '16' and pence '8' are clear and well aligned. Pounds column is blank/zero. The digits are bold and pass validation.

rows.description: To mr Osborn for lettering ye Hefe Eruditorum
rows.notes: Shillings '2' and pence '8' visible; the pence digit has a small flourish but is within valid range.

rows.description: For cleanyng ye yard behind ye Lodgings
rows.notes: Amount read as 2s 6d; both digits legible and in correct columns.

rows.description: Expences this year in ye Coll. Progresse
rows.notes: Shillings '6' is visible though the line is somewhat faint; taken as 6s 0d with moderate confidence.

rows.description: To mr Dodwell his bill for charges in recovering our Annuity from Thrup
rows.notes: Pounds '4' and shillings '5' are readable; pence column blank. The slightly heavy ink on the '4' suggests a firm reading.

rows.description: Letters about College business for six years past
rows.notes: Amount reads as £1 10s 0d; digits are clear and well aligned with the rule columns.

rows.description: To mr clark bookseller his bill
rows.notes: Shillings '7' visible; short line and clear numeric alignment.

Bottom right shows a final summed total, clearly written as '176.18' (interpreted as £176 18s 0d). The dot after 176 and the 18 are well aligned in the pounds and shillings columns; pence column appears zero.
